v2.subtitle: Advanced search is a small query language. Use field tokens, operators, and ranges to narrow results quickly.

basics.title: Basics (how queries are parsed)
basics.intro: Write conditions as field:value or field=value. Use commas for AND inside a group, and | to create OR groups.

text.title: Text fields (names & roles)
text.intro: These fields match text and are case-insensitive. Use : for partial match, = for exact match.

examples.mixTextAndNumeric:
song:hello, bpm:>150
Mix text + numeric constraints in one group.

numeric.title: Numeric fields (ranges & duration)
numeric.intro: Numeric fields support exact values and comparisons. Use >, <, >=, <= for ranges.

examples.tilesMin: tilecount:>=1000 filters levels with at least 1000 tiles.
examples.timeUnits:
time:>10m15s or time:<50s
Use h/m/s/ms units for chart length.

idsAndLinks.title: IDs & links (exact filters)
idsAndLinks.intro: Some fields are best used for exact matches or internal identifiers.

examples.idExact: id=1234 filters a specific level by numeric id.
examples.linkMatch: dlLink:drive.google matches download links by partial text. workshopLink:steamcommunity matches Steam Workshop URLs the same way.

tips.title: Tips
tips.intro: A few details that help avoid surprises.

bullets.caseInsensitive: Search is case-insensitive: 'SONG' and 'song' behave the same.
bullets.spacesAllowed: Spaces are allowed in values: song:Example Song works.
bullets.orderDoesNotMatter: Order does not matter within a group: artist:foo, song:bar is the same as song:bar, artist:foo.
bullets.aliasesMayMatch: Song/artist names may match known aliases, not just the primary display name.
bullets.groupingMentalModel: Think of it as (A AND B) OR (C AND D): commas build AND groups, | splits OR groups.

operatorColon.title: Partial match
operatorColon.desc: Use : to match partial text (example: song:go).

operatorComma.title: AND inside a group
operatorComma.desc: Separate terms with commas to require all conditions in the group.

operatorPipe.title: OR between groups
operatorPipe.desc: Use | to split your query into OR groups. If any group matches, the level is included.

operatorNot.title: NOT (exclude)
operatorNot.desc: Prefix a term with \! to exclude matches containing that term.

artist.title: Artist name
artist.desc: Filter by artist name (supports aliases and credited artists).

creator.title: Creator (any role)
creator.desc: Search across all creator roles (charter, vfx, etc.).

bpm.title: BPM
bpm.desc: Filter by BPM. Supports exact values and ranges.

tilecount.title: Tile count
tilecount.desc: Filter by total tiles. Supports exact values and ranges.

time.title: Chart length
time.desc: Filter by chart length. Use h/m/s/ms units or plain milliseconds.

workshopLink.title: Workshop link
workshopLink.desc: Filter by Steam Workshop (or other workshop) URL text, same wildcard rules as dlLink.

legacyDllink.title: Legacy download link
legacyDllink.desc: Filter by legacy download link text (older entries).

search.title: Field-Specific Search
search.description: You can search for specific fields using two different operators: colon (:) for partial matches and equals (=) for exact matches.

examples.title: Available Fields
examples.creatorField: Search by creator name across all roles (e.g., 'creator:Nep' or 'creator=Nephrolepis')

numeric.title: BPM, tile count, and chart length
numeric.description: Filter by BPM (bpm:), tile count (tilecount:), or chart length in milliseconds (time:). For time:, use h, m, s, or ms units (e.g. 10m15s, 50s), or a plain number as milliseconds. Prefix with >, <, >=, or <= for ranges. Separate multiple conditions with commas (AND) within the same search group.

examples.title: Numeric examples
examples.bpmRange: BPM strictly between 100 and 200 (use >= or <= if you want inclusive bounds)
examples.timeComposite: Levels longer than 10 minutes 15 seconds (chart length)
examples.timeMsPlain: Levels longer than 130000 ms (plain number = milliseconds)
examples.combine: Combine with song/artist filters, e.g. song:hello, bpm:>150

operators.title: Search Operators
operators.description: You can combine multiple search conditions using operators.

examples.title: Operator Examples
examples.and: Search for levels that match both conditions (AND). This will look for charts of 'hello' by 'camellia'
examples.or: Search for levels that match either condition (OR). This searches for either goTe charts (without EX) or any XNOR charts
examples.complex: Complex search combining AND and OR conditions. This looks for 'onus' charts by 'gazizi' or any charts with song by 'ludicin'
examples.not: Usage of '\!' will exclude results containing the term - this expression will exclude 'nerfed' onus charts

points.caseSensitive: Searches are case-insensitive - 'SONG' and 'song' will give the same results
points.partial: Colon (:) allows partial matches - 'song:Ex' will match 'Example'
points.exact: Equals (=) requires exact matches - 'song=Example' will only match 'Example'
points.spaces: Spaces are allowed in search terms - 'song:Example Song' works
points.order: The order of conditions doesn't matter within each group
points.roles: Use 'creator' to search across all roles, or 'charter'/'vfxer' for specific roles
